allow: Bash(git -C /Users/paultaku/Projects/dev-scheduler add .gitignore config.yaml.example dev_scheduler/config.py dev_scheduler/scheduler.py dev_scheduler/yaml_config.py pyproject.toml uv.lock)

Bash(git -C /Users/paultaku/Projects/dev-scheduler commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
add YAML config for mapping Notion database IDs to local working directories

Adds a config.yaml system that maps Notion database IDs to local project
paths, replacing the need for per-task ProjectPath Notion properties.
The scheduler resolves working directories from YAML first, falling back
to the task''s ProjectPath property for backwards compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/paultaku/Projects/dev-scheduler commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix task getting stuck on AI failure by reverting to trigger status

When AI processing fails after all retries, the task status is now
reverted to its original trigger status \(ToPlan/ReadyToImplement\)
instead of being left in the in-progress state. This allows the
scheduler to pick up and retry the task on subsequent poll cycles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/paultaku/Projects/dev-scheduler commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
make notion_database_id optional, resolve from config.yaml

NOTION_DATABASE_ID env var is no longer required. If not set, the
scheduler resolves it from the first project entry in config.yaml.
A clear error is raised if neither source provides a database ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
